BINDING ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL – PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC.
Binding Arbitration Agreement
LOAN 4222135160 / PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. / 02/23/2024
This Binding Arbitration Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between William Gray ("you" or "Borrower") and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. ("we," "us," or "Lender") in connection with this loan identified as loan number 4222135160 and any account, transaction, or relationship arising therefrom. By signing the related loan documents or otherwise accepting the terms set forth herein, you acknowledge that you have read this Agreement, that you understand it materially affects your legal rights, and that you agree to resolve Covered Disputes through individual binding arbitration rather than in court before a judge or jury. Please read this Agreement carefully and retain a copy for your records. Capitalized terms not defined herein have the meanings given in the loan documents.
1. AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE
You and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. agree that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, any Covered Dispute (as defined below) shall be resolved through arbitration on an individual basis and not in a court, or by class, collective, or representative action, at the election of either party. This Agreement fulfills the intention of the parties to resolve disputes without undue delay or expense.
Your right to make a claim against the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. § 1 et seq. (the "FAA"), and you agree that any arbitration will be conducted by and in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association (the "AAA") under its Consumer Arbitration Rules, which may be found at 89266 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaysville, UT 84084, or by requesting a copy from the AAA.
2. DISPUTES COVERED
A "Covered Dispute" means any claim, dispute, or controversy of any kind, whether based in contract, tort, statute, regulation, common law, equity, or otherwise, that arises out of or relates in any way to the loan, or the relationship between the parties.

loan, the loan documents, this Agreement, the property securing the loan, the origination, underwriting, processing, closing, funding, servicing, collection, or enforcement of the loan, or any communication, disclosure, advertisement, product, or service associated with the relationship between you and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. The term is to be constructed in the broadest manner permitted by law.

Covered Disputes also include, without limitation, claims relating to the validity, enforceability, scope, or interpretation of this Agreement, except an expressly reserved for judicial determination herein; claims regarding alleged fraud, misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, or unfair or deceptive acts or practices; claims under federal or state consumer-protection, lending, or debt collection statutes; and claims arising from any prior loan, account, or transaction between you and the Lender. Claims by or against the Lender’s affiliates, agents, employees, officers, directors, successors, assigns, and servicers are likewise included.

The fact that a Covered Dispute may also constitute a claim by or against a third party shall not defeat the obligation to arbitrate as between you and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. If any portion of a dispute falls within the scope of this Agreement and any portion does not, the parties shall arbitrate the portion that is covered and may stay the remaining portion due to the arbitration, to the extent permitted by applicable law and the rules of the administering body.

3. DISPUTES EXCLUDED
Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require arbitration of the following matters: any action by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., to foreclose upon, or to obtain possession of, the property securing the loan; any judicial or non-judicial foreclosure, power of sale, deed in lieu, equipment, or similar lien, or any other legal documents or actions pertinent to such; the exercise of any other Lender rights including, without limitation, preliminary injunctions, restitution, receivership, or other interim relief; and any claims that are not otherwise subject to arbitration pursuant to applicable law.

This Agreement does not preclude either party from seeking governmental, regulatory, or other intervention from any federal, state, or local government agency in relation to any consumer-related claims, nor does it require arbitration of any matter in which an authority from exercising enforcement actions. 

4. SMALL-CLAIMS CARVE-OUT
Either you or Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may elect to bring and pursue an individual claim in a small-claims court (or competent jurisdiction in lieu of arbitration), provided that the claim qualifies for that court under its jurisdictional limits and remains in that forum on an individual basis. This carve-out is intended

to preserve a simple, low-cost venue for the resolution of modest individual disputes without the procedures associated with formal arbitration or litigation. 

If a claim filed in small-claims court is transferred, removed, appealed to, or otherwise proceeds in a court other than a small-claims court, or if the claim is amended to seek relief or to assert allegations that excluded jurisdiction in the small-claims court, then either party may thereafter proceed to compel arbitration of that claim under this Agreement. Any such election shall be honored by the parties, and the dispute shall proceed in arbitration in accordance with the terms set forth herein. 

1. ADMINISTERING BODY AND RULES  
Unless the parties agree in writing to a different forum, a Covered Dispute submitted to arbitration shall be administered by a nationally recognized, neutral arbitration administrator that maintains established procedures for the resolution of consumer disputes, in accordance with the administrator’s consumer arbitration rules in effect at the time the arbitration is initiated. If this agreement provides for the arbitration to be conducted under a specific arbitration administrator’s rules, then the arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with such established rules for the stated claims. 

To the extent the chosen administrator's rules conflict with this Agreement, this Agreement shall govern. The arbitrator shall be the sole judge of the facts and law applicable to any dispute between the parties. 

You may contact an administrator’s representative to determine the arbitrability of any Covered Dispute and to resolve any dispute as to the procedure employed, applicability, or enforceability of this Agreement. 

By signing this Agreement, you and we agree that the arbitrator shall have all of the substantive law authorities to determine and resolve any individual claims or disputes. You may contact a administrator’s representative by contacting Plaza Mortgage, Inc., 286.739.3411.317 or in writing at 7981 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Bend, Indiana, IN 02793. 

5. EFFECT OF ARBITRATION; JURY-TRIAL WAIVER  
BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATE COVERED DISPUTES, YOU AND PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. EACH KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVE THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY AND THE RIGHT TO HAVE COVERED DISPUTES DECIDED BY A JUDGE IN A COURT OF LAW. 

ARBITRATION OF COVERED DISPUTES CONDUCTED PURSUANT TO THIS AGREEMENT IS IN LIEU OF ANY COURT PROCEEDING TO THE SAME EXTENT AS A MATERIAL INDUCEMENT FOR THE LOAN TRANSACTION AND EACH RATIFIES THAT YOU CONTINUE TO CONSULT WITH LEGAL COUNSEL OF ITS OWN CHOOSING REGARDING THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS WAIVER.

In arbitration, the proceedings are generally more streamlined than litigation, discovery may be more limited, and the rights to pre-trial and appellate review are substantially narrower than those available in court. The arbitrator's decision is final and binding, subject only to the limited grounds for review provided under the FAA. Judgment on the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction. The parties accept these features of arbitration in exchange for its efficiency, finality, and reduced cost. The arbitrator may award any relief, including monetary damages, declaratory relief, injunctive relief, and statutory remedies, that a court could award to an individual party under applicable law, and only in favor of the individual party seeking relief and only to the extent necessary to provide relief warranted by that party's individual claim. The arbitrator shall have a reasoned written award setting forth the essential findings and conclusions on which the award is based, in accordance with the administrator's rules. 1. CLASS-ACTION AND REPRESENTATIVE WAIVER COVERED DISPUTES SHALL BE ARBITRATED ONLY ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS. YOU AND PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. EACH WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO ARBITRATE OR LITIGATE ANY COVERED DISPUTE AS A CLASS ACTION, COLLECTIVE ACTION, PRIVATE ATTORNEY-GENERAL ACTION, OR OTHER REPRESENTATIVE PROCEEDING. OR TO SERVE AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE OR CLASS MEMBER, OR TO PARTICIPATE AS A MEMBER OF A CLASS OF CLAIMANTS. The arbitrator shall have no authority to consolidate or join the claims of more than one person and may not prescribe any form of class, collective, or representative proceeding. The parties agree that this class-action and representative waiver is a fundamental and non-severable element of the parties' agreement to arbitrate. If any court or arbitrator determines that the unwaivable nature of this provision contradicts any rule of arbitrability or unwaivable condition uniquely applicable to a particular Covered Dispute or claim for relief, then to that extent, that specific provision shall be deemed to have been placed on a conditional basis and shall remain subject to arbitration, but covered disputes and claims shall remain subject to arbitration on an individual basis pursuant to this Agreement. No arbitration involving this Agreement shall be joined or consolidated with any other arbitration involving a different dispute, nor shall any arbitration award or decision be used as precedential authority in any other arbitration, except as may be required by law. The parties further agree to arbitrate any disputes concerning the interpretation or enforcement of this Agreement in accordance with its terms and conditions and no other provision of this Agreement is intended to be joined or consolidated with any other arbitration. 1. COSTS AND FEES Your party initiating arbitration shall pay the filing fee required by the administrator, except that, upon your written demand, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. shall reimburse you for half of such filing fee, or the total filing fee, if your arbitration claim is for $10,000 or less. If you and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. agree to compel arbitration, the losing party shall pay the costs and expenses of the arbitration, and if no agreement exists then that party shall pay the costs of initiating arbitration or the costs of pursuing the claim. Such agreement does not preclude any party from seeking to recover their own costs and expenses. Each party shall bear their own attorneys' fees, expert fees, and other costs incurred in connection with the arbitration, except that the arbitrator may award such costs to the prevailing party to the extent such an award would be available under the applicable substantive law.

governing the Covered Dispute, or as otherwise provided by the loan documents. The allocation of fees and costs shall be set forth in the arbitrator's award and is subject to the same finality as the remainder of the award.

If the arbitrator determines that a claim or defense was frivolous, asserted in bad faith, or brought for an improper purpose, the arbitrator may reallocate the fees and costs of the arbitration as prohibited by the arbitrator's rules and applicable law. Nothing in this section limits any statutory right you may have to recover fees and costs that you would be entitled to recover had the Covered Dispute been adjudicated in court.

1. LOCATION AND CONDUCT OF ARBITRATION

Unless the parties agree otherwise in writing, any in-person arbitration hearing shall be held in the federal judicial district that includes your residence or, where applicable, the VT county in which the property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kalyatchek, VT 08404 is situated. The arbitrator may, consistent with the administrator's rules, conduct proceedings by telephone, videoconference, or on the basis of written submissions where appropriate, taking into account the convenience of the parties and witnesses, and the interest in minimizing the cost and burden of the proceeding.

The arbitration shall be conducted in the English language. The arbitrator shall manage the exchange of information in a manner proportionate to the dispute, permitting reasonable discovery consistent with the desire to make arbitration efficient and economical while allowing each party a fair opportunity to present their case. The arbitrator shall have broad discretion to supervise the arbitration process and to impose appropriate sanctions, where necessary, including the ability to impose the cost of the arbitration on the parties, in accordance with the arbitrator's rules and applicable law.

Except as may be required by law, to enforce or challenge the award, or to seek interim relief, the parties shall maintain the confidentiality of the arbitration proceedings, including the existence, content, and results of such arbitration. You and your advisers who have a need to know and who agree to be bound by this confidentiality obligation.

10. RIGHT TO OPT OUT

You have the right to reject this Agreement and to opt out of arbitration without affecting any other term of the Agreement or your ability to apply for credit. To opt out, you must send Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. written notice at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kalyatchek, VT 08404, and to the attention of the Compliance Department. The notice must be dated and sent to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096. Such notice must include your name, address, and the date you signed or authorized other ways to avoid being bound by this Agreement. Your decision not to opt out will not apply to you, but all other terms of the loan documents will remain in full force and effect.

Only the notice described in this section, sent within the sixty (60) day period and in the form specified, will be effective to opt out of arbitration. Continued performance under the loan, the use of any account, or the absence of a timely opt-out notice will be deemed your acceptance of this Agreement. We recommend that you retain proof of mailing your opt-out notice for your records and that you contact us at 296.739.3411.17 if you have questions regarding this right.  I. SEVERABILITY AND SURVIVAL Except as otherwise provided in this section, the class-action and representative waiver in Section 7, if any portion of this Agreement is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court or arbitrator of competent jurisdiction, that portion shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced to the fullest extent permitted by law as if the severed portion had not been included. The invalidity of any provision in one jurisdiction shall not affect the validity or enforceability of that provision in any other jurisdiction. This Agreement shall survive the repayment, prepayment, refinancing, modification, satisfaction, charge-off, or cancellation of the loan, the sale, assignment, or transfer of the loan by you, or your successor, assignee, or servicer; the closing or termination of any account or relationship between you and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc; and the bankruptcy, insolvency, or dissolution of either party to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law. The provisions of this Agreement run to the benefit of and bind upon the parties and their respective heirs, successors, assigns, and permitted Transferees. This Agreement, together with the arbitration-related provisions of the loan documents, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the arbitration of Covered Disputes and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understanding on that subject. No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by both parties. By proceeding with the loan transaction identified by loan number 422315360, dated February 23, 2024, you acknowledge your understanding and agreement to the terms set forth herein. ACKNOWLEDGED BY BORROWER William Gray

PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. - GENERAL DISCLOSURES AND BORROWER LENDER Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.
ACKNOWLEDGMENTS
LOAN NO. 4222315360
General Disclosures and Acknowledgments
DATE 02/23/2024
Issued to WIllIam Gray BORROWER William Gray
NOTICE & DISCLOSURE
This consolidated General Disclosures and Borrower Acknowledgments document is furnished by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to William Gray in connection with
the loan identified as 4222315360 and secured by the real property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040 in the State of VT.
The disclosures and certifications set forth herein are provided to satisfy applicable federal and state requirements and to memorialize the understandings of the
parties as of February 23, 2024. By reviewing and executing this document, William Gray acknowledges receipt of the notices described below and affirms the
accuracy of the representations made in the loan application and supporting materials. Capitalized placeholder terms are completed elsewhere within the loan
package and incorporated herein by reference for all purposes.
§ 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
This document consolidates several distinct borrower notices, certifications, and acknowledgments that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is required or elects to provide in
connection with the origination of the loan referenced above. It is intended to operate together with, and not in substitution for, the promissory note, security instrument,
and all other disclosures delivered to William Gray. Where any provision of this document conflicts with a more specific disclosure mandated by applicable law, the more
specific disclosure shall control to the extent of the conflict, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect without impairment.
Each section below addresses a separate subject matter and may be relied upon independently. The headings are provided solely for convenience and ease of reference and
do not limit, expand, or otherwise affect the substance of the acknowledgments that follow. William Gray understands that signing this document does not obligate Plaza
Home Mortgage, Inc. to close or fund the loan and does not waive any condition that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may impose prior to closing. Nothing herein creates any
commitment except as expressly stated in a separately executed written commitment from Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc..
William Gray acknowledges that this document has been made available in a manner permitting review prior to execution, and that William Gray has had a reasonable
opportunity to read it, to ask questions of Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., and to seek independent advice from counsel or other advisors of William Gray's choosing. The
execution of this document reflects William Gray's informed and voluntary agreement to the matters described herein as of the date set forth above.
§ 2. RIGHT TO RECEIVE APPRAISAL
In connection with an application for a loan to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is required to provide William Gray with a copy of each
written appraisal and valuation developed in connection with the application. William Gray will be provided a copy promptly upon completion, or no later than the period
required by applicable law before the loan closes, whichever is earlier, even if the transaction does not close or is not consummated. This right exists regardless of whether
William Gray requests a copy and is provided at no additional charge for the copy itself.
William Gray may be charged for the reasonable cost of any appraisal or valuation obtained, and such cost may be disclosed separately within the closing documents and
settlement statement applicable to the loan. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may require William Gray to pay for an appraisal as a condition of processing the application.
Receipt of a copy of any appraisal does not entitle William Gray to rely upon it for any purpose other than the credit transaction, and the appraisal remains the property of
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. unless otherwise required by law.
By executing this document, William Gray acknowledges that the right to receive a copy of any appraisal or valuation has been disclosed and understood. William Gray
may obtain additional information regarding this right by contacting Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 296.739.3411x317 or by writing to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891
Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793. William Gray further understands that William Gray may waive the timing requirement for delivery in certain
circumstances permitted by law, provided any such waiver is made in a separate writing and does not affect William Gray's underlying right to receive the copy.
§ 3. ACCURACY OF INFORMATION
William Gray certifies that all statements, documents, and information provided to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in connection with the loan application, including but not
limited to information regarding income, employment, assets, liabilities, credit history, and the subject property, are true, complete, and accurate as of the date provided.
William Gray understands that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is relying upon the accuracy of this information in evaluating the application, in making its credit decision, and
in determining the terms upon which the loan may be offered. Any material misstatement or omission may constitute grounds for denial or for declaring the loan in default
after closing.
William Gray agrees to notify Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. promptly, in writing, of any material change in the information previously furnished that occurs at any time
before the loan is closed and funded. Such changes include, without limitation, a change in employment status, a material reduction in income, the incurrence of new debt,
or a change in the intended use or occupancy of the subject property. William Gray acknowledges that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may verify any information provided
through third parties, consumer reporting agencies, employers, financial institutions, and other appropriate sources at any time.
William Gray acknowledges that intentionally or negligently making a false statement to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. or to a federally related mortgage lender may
constitute a violation of applicable federal and state law and may subject William Gray to civil and criminal penalties, including fines and imprisonment. William Gray
affirms that William Gray has reviewed the information submitted and that it accurately reflects William Gray's financial condition and the facts surrounding the requested
transaction as of February 23, 2024.
§ 4. OCCUPANCY CERTIFICATION
William Gray certifies the intended occupancy status of the subject property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040 as represented in the
loan application. If the property has been represented as William Gray's principal residence, William Gray certifies an intention to occupy, establish, and use the property as
William Gray's principal residence within the period customarily required following closing, and to continue to occupy the property as a principal residence for the period
required by the loan documents, unless otherwise agreed by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in writing or required by extenuating circumstances beyond William Gray's
reasonable control.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

William Gray understands that the occupancy representation is a material factor in Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.'s underwriting decision and in the pricing and terms of the
loan, and that loans secured by a principal residence may carry different terms than loans secured by second homes or investment properties. William Gray agrees that any
change in the intended occupancy of the property prior to closing must be disclosed promptly to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., and acknowledges that such a change may
affect William Gray's eligibility for the loan or the terms upon which it is offered.
William Gray acknowledges that a false certification regarding occupancy may constitute mortgage fraud and may result in acceleration of the loan, civil liability, and
criminal prosecution under applicable law. By executing this document, William Gray affirms that the occupancy status stated in the application is accurate and reflects
William Gray's genuine intention as of the date hereof, and that William Gray will promptly inform Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. should that intention change before the
loan is consummated.
§ 5. INTENT TO PROCEED
Following delivery of the Loan Estimate or other early disclosures applicable to the loan, William Gray may indicate an intent to proceed with the transaction. Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc. will not impose any fee on William Gray in connection with the application, other than a reasonable fee for obtaining William Gray's consumer report where
permitted, until after William Gray has received the applicable early disclosures and has communicated an intent to proceed with the transaction. An indication of intent to
proceed does not obligate William Gray to accept the loan and does not constitute a commitment by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to extend credit.
William Gray may communicate intent to proceed by any reasonable means, including in person, by telephone at 296.739.3411x317, by electronic communication, or in
writing addressed to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793, in each case in a manner that clearly identifies the loan
referenced as 4222315360. Silence or inaction shall not be construed as an indication of intent to proceed. William Gray understands that the interest rate and certain other
terms disclosed in the early disclosures are not locked or guaranteed unless and until a separate rate-lock agreement is executed in accordance with Plaza Home Mortgage,
Inc.'s requirements.
William Gray acknowledges that William Gray retains the right to decline to proceed with the transaction at any time prior to consummation, subject to forfeiture only of
fees already incurred and permitted under applicable law. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. likewise reserves the right to decline to extend credit if any condition of approval is
not satisfied. Nothing in this section shall be deemed to waive any right of rescission that may apply to the transaction under federal or state law.
§ 6. ANTI-STEERING NOTICE
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. and any mortgage loan originator acting on its behalf are prohibited by applicable law from steering William Gray toward a loan in order to
increase the originator's compensation, unless the loan is in William Gray's interest. Loan originator compensation may not be based on the terms of the loan other than the
amount of credit extended, and William Gray is entitled to be offered loan options for which William Gray likely qualifies without regard to the compensation the
originator may receive. This notice is provided in general terms and does not itself constitute an offer of any particular loan product.
William Gray acknowledges that William Gray has been or will be presented with information sufficient to make an informed decision among loan options for which
William Gray qualifies, which may include options with the lowest available interest rate, options with the lowest total points and origination fees, and options without
certain risky features, to the extent such options are available and consistent with William Gray's stated objectives. William Gray understands that not all products are
available to every applicant and that eligibility depends upon underwriting and program guidelines applicable to the loan.
Any questions concerning the loan options available to William Gray, the basis on which a particular product was recommended, or the compensation practices applicable
to the transaction may be directed to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 296.739.3411x317. William Gray acknowledges receipt of this anti-steering notice and affirms that
William Gray has had the opportunity to inquire about alternative loan products prior to selecting the loan referenced herein.
§ 7. AFFILIATED BUSINESS NOTICE
William Gray is hereby notified, in general terms, that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may have a business relationship with one or more providers of settlement services,
which may include affiliated entities under common ownership or control with Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.. Because of these relationships, the referral of William Gray to
such a provider may provide Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. or its affiliates with a financial or other benefit. This notice is provided so that William Gray may make an
informed decision regarding the selection of settlement service providers in connection with the loan transaction.
Except where use of a particular provider is required by law or by the terms of the loan, William Gray is generally not required to use any affiliated or referred provider as
a condition of obtaining the loan, and William Gray is free to shop for and select settlement service providers of William Gray's own choosing. William Gray is encouraged
to compare the prices, services, and reputation of available providers to determine that William Gray is receiving the best value. The estimated charges of any affiliated
provider, where applicable, are or will be set forth in the disclosures specific to that provider.
William Gray acknowledges that this affiliated business arrangement notice has been provided in general terms and that any specific affiliated relationship affecting the
loan, together with the range of charges for the services involved, will be disclosed separately as required by applicable law. William Gray may obtain further information
regarding Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.'s business relationships by contacting Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793 or
296.739.3411x317.
§ 8. FLOOD INSURANCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will determine whether the subject property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040 is situated within an area
designated as a special flood hazard area pursuant to applicable federal standards. If the property is so located and is in a community participating in the applicable federal
flood insurance program, federal law requires that adequate flood insurance be maintained on the improvements securing the loan for the term of the loan. William Gray
acknowledges receipt of, or the right to receive, any required notice concerning special flood hazards and the availability of federal disaster relief assistance.
William Gray understands that, if flood insurance is required, the coverage must be maintained in an amount at least equal to the outstanding principal balance of the loan
or the maximum limit of coverage available under the applicable program, whichever is less, subject to any greater amount required by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. or
applicable law. Should William Gray fail to maintain required flood insurance, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may, after providing any notice required by law, purchase such
insurance and charge the cost to William Gray, and such force-placed coverage may be more expensive and may provide less protection than coverage William Gray could
obtain independently.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

William Gray acknowledges that flood zone determinations may change over time and that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may obtain updated determinations, including life-
of-loan monitoring, during the term of the loan. William Gray agrees to cooperate with Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in obtaining and maintaining any required coverage and
to provide evidence of such coverage upon request. Questions regarding flood insurance requirements may be directed to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 296.739.3411x317.
§ 9. HAZARD INSURANCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT
William Gray agrees to obtain and maintain, at William Gray's expense, hazard or property insurance covering the improvements on the subject property against loss by
fire, hazards included within the term extended coverage, and such other hazards as Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may reasonably require, in amounts and for periods that
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. requires consistent with applicable law and the terms of the security instrument. Such insurance must be carried with one or more insurers
acceptable to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. and must name Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. as mortgagee or loss payee, as applicable, with appropriate provisions for notice to
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. prior to cancellation or material change.
William Gray understands that the insurance must remain in effect continuously from the date of closing and throughout the term of the loan, and that William Gray is
responsible for the timely payment of all premiums, whether paid directly by William Gray or through an escrow account established and maintained by Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc.. If William Gray fails to maintain the required coverage, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may obtain force-placed coverage protecting only Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc.'s interest, the cost of which may be added to the loan balance and which may be substantially more costly and less protective than coverage William Gray
could obtain.
William Gray acknowledges that the selection of an insurance provider is generally William Gray's choice, subject to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.'s reasonable requirements
regarding the financial strength and acceptability of the insurer. William Gray agrees to furnish Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. with evidence of required hazard insurance
prior to closing and to deliver renewal evidence as coverage periods expire, so that there is no lapse in protection for the property securing the loan referenced as
4222315360.
§ 10. ELECTRONIC RECORDS AND COMMUNICATIONS
To the extent William Gray has separately consented to the use of electronic records and signatures, William Gray acknowledges that certain disclosures, including this
document, may be provided in electronic form and that William Gray's electronic signature shall have the same force and effect as a handwritten signature for all purposes
permitted by law. William Gray represents that William Gray has the hardware and software necessary to access and retain electronic records and that William Gray has
been advised of the right to withdraw such consent and to obtain paper copies upon request, subject to any applicable fee disclosed by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc..
Where electronic delivery is used, a disclosure is deemed received when it is made available to William Gray in a manner reasonably designed to provide notice and
access. William Gray agrees to maintain a current and valid electronic address on file with Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. and to notify Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. promptly
of any change. Nothing in this section requires William Gray to accept electronic delivery, and William Gray may continue to receive paper disclosures by so notifying
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793 or 296.739.3411x317.
William Gray understands that records relating to the loan, however delivered, should be retained by William Gray for William Gray's records, and that Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc. will retain copies in accordance with its record-retention policies and applicable law. William Gray's consent to electronic records, if given, applies only to
the categories of records described in the separate consent and does not alter the substance of any disclosure or acknowledgment contained herein.
§ 11. EQUAL CREDIT OPPORTUNITY
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is prohibited by federal law from discriminating against any credit applicant on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital
status, age (provided the applicant has the capacity to contract), the fact that all or part of the applicant's income derives from a public assistance program, or the fact that
the applicant has in good faith exercised any right under the applicable consumer credit protection statutes. William Gray acknowledges that the credit decision on the loan
referenced herein has been or will be made without regard to any such prohibited basis.
If William Gray's application for credit is denied, William Gray has the right to a written statement of the specific reasons for the denial, which William Gray may request
within the period prescribed by law. William Gray may obtain such a statement, or further information regarding William Gray's rights, by contacting Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc. at 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793 or 296.739.3411x317. The federal agency that administers compliance with these laws as to
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is identified in the separate notices furnished with the credit decision or available upon request.
William Gray acknowledges that any income from alimony, child support, or separate maintenance payments need not be disclosed unless William Gray chooses to have it
considered in evaluating the application. This acknowledgment is provided for informational purposes and does not constitute a credit decision or an admission of
eligibility or ineligibility for any particular loan product offered by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc..
§ 12. PRIVACY AND INFORMATION SHARING
In the course of processing and servicing the loan, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. collects nonpublic personal information about William Gray from the application, from
consumer reporting agencies, and from William Gray's transactions with Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. and others. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. maintains physical, electronic,
and procedural safeguards designed to protect this information and restricts access to those who require it to provide products or services to William Gray. The specific
categories of information collected and the circumstances under which it may be shared are described in Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.'s separate privacy notice furnished to
William Gray.
William Gray acknowledges that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may disclose information about William Gray and the loan to third parties as permitted or required by law,
including to service providers, investors, insurers, regulators, and others involved in originating, funding, securing, selling, or servicing the loan. William Gray understands
that certain sharing may be subject to William Gray's right to opt out, where applicable, and that the manner of exercising any such right is described in the privacy notice.
This acknowledgment does not enlarge or diminish the disclosures contained in that notice.

This Consumer Account Terms and Conditions Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by and between William Gray ("Borrower") and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. ("Lender"), as of the date of the last signature below. You may borrow, repay, and borrow again as often as you like, subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. The Agreement supersedes any previous agreements you may have had with Lender regarding this Account.

1. DEFINITIONS
As used in this Agreement, certain capitalized terms carry the meanings assigned to them in this section and the terms primarily regulate relations. "Account" means any account, loan, line of credit, or similar extended to you by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. "Borrower" means you, the person or entity that is entering into this Agreement. "Lender" refers to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., its successors, and assigns. "Loan Documents" means this Agreement and any additional documents required by Lender.

2. PROMISE TO PAY
You promise to pay to the order of Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., or its subsequent holder of the Loan Documents, the full amount of the established loan(s) and all interest charges thereon.

3. APPLICATION OF PAYMENTS
Unless otherwise specified in addition, we will apply each payment to remove your secured cost and interest prior to outstanding principal.

4. LATE CHARGES AND FEES
If any scheduled payment is not paid by the last day of the grace period described in your Loan Documents, you agree to pay the maximum late fee allowable.

5. DEFAULT
You will default under this Agreement if you become unable to pay your obligations as they become due, fail to perform any obligation under the Agreement, or if your loan is not self-sustained or increased.

1.0 REMEDIES UPON DEFAULT  
Upon the occurrence of a default that remains uncured beyond applicable cure period, we may, at our option and in the extent permitted by Applicable Law, declare all amounts payable under the applicable agreement or instruments immediately due and payable, and any amounts you owe us may accrue interest at the highest rate permitted by Applicable Law, define possible penalties, and exercise any rights or remedies available to us hereunder or at law.

In addition to the rights and remedies of us as described above, we may also exercise the right to withdraw or deny any services rendered to you during the default.

2.0 CHANGES TO TERMS  
We may amend, modify, or restate some of this Agreement from time to time in the extent permitted by Applicable Law. Where a change materially impacts your rights or obligations hereunder, we will provide you notice of such change. Consistent with Applicable Law, you will be deemed to have agreed and accepted any changes to these terms unless you notify us otherwise in writing.

3.0 GOVERNING LAW  
This Agreement and its rights and obligations arising under shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of VT and, where applicable, the federal laws of the United States of America. You agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Vermont and the federal courts of the United States located in that state.

4.0 SEVERABILITY  
If any provision of this Agreement, or the application of any provision to any person or circumstances, is held to be invalid or unenforceable under jurisdiction laws, the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect. If any invalid or unenforceable provision is capable of being made valid and enforceable, then such provision shall be construed to be modified so as to be valid and enforceable to the maximum extent permitted by Applicable Law.

5.0 ASSIGNMENT  
You may not assign, delegate, or otherwise convey this Agreement, nor its terms, rights or obligations under this Agreement to any other party without our prior written consent. As a condition of such assignment, the assignee must be capable of assuming and performing the obligations under this Agreement. You may not assign any right or obligation under this Agreement without obtaining our prior express written consent.

6.0 NOTICES  
Except where Applicable Law specifies a different method, notice that you are required or permitted to give under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the other party at the address specified next to the parties' signatures below. Notice shall be deemed to have been given when it has been delivered by hand, sent via certified mail, return receipt requested, or by recognized overnight delivery service. You should not use email for delivery of notices.

If you have been presented with this Acc Agreement, noting prior to you shall consider revoke or all of the provisions of this Agreement. 12. NO WAIVER

No indulgence given by either party hereunder shall extend to any other transaction or action of either party. The execution of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of any rights or obligations of the parties under the same or any other agreement.



13. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. • PRIVACY NOTICE • WHAT WE DO WITH YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION  Privacy Notice  LOAN 4222313560 / PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. / 02/23/2024  This Privacy Notice describes how Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. ("Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.," "we," "us," or "our") collects, shares, and protects the personal information of consumers and customers in connection with the loan identified as 4222313560 and the related residential financing secured by the property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaysville, VT 0840. Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law gives consumers the right to limit some but not all sharing. This notice also explains the choices available to you and how you may exercise them. Please read it carefully and retain a copy with your loan records. The provisions of this notice apply throughout the life of your account and after the relationship ends, except as otherwise required or permitted by law.  1. WHY WE PROVIDE THIS NOTICE  Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and its implementing regulations, requires us to tell you how we collect, share, and protect that information. We are providing this notice so that you understand how and when we share your information. When you apply for a loan, the personal information and the product or service you have requested will depend on the number of accounts you maintain and the credit products you have.  Additionally, we’ll provide you with updates at least annually on your right to opt-out. If your account is closed or inactive, we will send you a notice, on a periodic basis for our records, outlining your continuing right to opt-out.  When you apply for a loan or continue to share your information as described in this notice, this means that you are acknowledging that we have implemented and will continue to implement our privacy practices in accordance with the law and our other agreements between you and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. or under the relevant state laws. If state laws provide you additional rights, please let us know so we can respond to your communications with us by telephone, in writing, or electronically.  2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT  We collect personal information about you in connection with providing you a financial product or service. The personal information we collect depends on the product or service you apply for and may include your name, address, phone number, Social Security number and other forms of personal identification.

We also collect your personal information from others, such as credit bureaus, affiliates, and other companies. For example, we may receive your credit history, creditworthiness, and credit scores from consumer reporting agencies, and we may receive verification of employment, deposits, and other account information from third parties you authorize. We maintain records relating to the origination, servicing, and, where applicable, the transfer of your loan, including documents you sign at closing and information generated during the life of your account.

The categories of information we collect and the manner in which we use that information may change over time as products, services, and legal requirements evolve. Where required, we will update this privacy policy and notify you of any material changes in our information practices. We collect only the personal information reasonably necessary to provide the products and services you request, to administer your account, and to comply with our legal and regulatory obligations.

1. HOW WE SHARE YOUR INFORMATION

All financial companies need to share customers' personal information to run their everyday business. In the section that follows, we describe the reasons financial companies can share their customers' personal information, the reasons Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. choose to share that information, and whether you can limit sharing. Whether you can limit sharing depends on the reason for which the information is shared and the protections afforded to you by federal and state laws.

We may share your personal information for our everyday business purposes, such as to process your transactions, maintain your account, respond to court orders and legal investigations, or report to credit bureaus. We may also share personal information necessary to offer you products and services, to you, and offer programs to enhance your customer experience or facilitate agreements. For this section, sharing may occur without your ability to limit it, except where federal or state law provides otherwise.

We may further share your personal information for purposes related to our affiliates' everyday business, including information about your transactions and experiences with us or our affiliates, information about your creditworthiness, and information we obtain from nonaffiliated third parties relating to your credit profile. Sharing for nonaffiliated third parties does not require your consent and is more fully described in the sections of this notice addressing nonaffiliated third parties, subsidiaries, and your rights.

2. REASONS YOU CANNOT LIMIT SHARING

There are certain everyday business purposes for which federal law does not permit you to limit our sharing of your personal information. These include sharing for everyday business purposes, such as: to process your transactions, maintain your account, respond to court orders, subpoenas, regulatory inquiries, and, if necessary, to protect our interest or our rights and property. Sharing for general business purposes includes information we share with affiliates, third parties performing services on our behalf, including third-party vendors engaged in transactions processing, account servicing, mailing, collection, marketing, and research. We also share as required to comply with federal, state, and local laws regarding our liabilities.  
We maintain the confidentiality and security of your information and use it only for the limited purposes for which it was

1. SHARING WITH AFFILIATES  
Affiliates are companies related to us by common ownership or control, and they can include both financial and nonfinancial companies. We may share with our affiliates information about your transactions and experiences with us, such as your account history and payment record. This type of information sharing is generally required by law to opt out, although we will honor any additional limitations required under applicable state law.  

We may also share among our affiliates certain other information about you, including information we obtain from your application and from consumer reporting agencies relating to your creditworthiness. Federal law gives you the right to limit the sharing of this category of information so that our affiliates may not use it to market their products and services to you. If you wish to limit such sharing, you may do so by following the instructions set out in the section of this notice describing your choices and how to contact us.  

Any election you make to limit affiliate sharing will apply to the joint account holders associated with loan 4222135360 but will remain in effect unless you instruct otherwise. Your election will remain in effect until you notify us of a change, and we will apply your choice within a reasonable period after we receive and process your request.  

2. SHARING WITH NONAFFILIATES  
Nonaffiliates are companies not related to us by common ownership or control, and they can also include both financial and nonfinancial companies. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. does not share your personal information with nonaffiliates for them to market their own products and services to you, except as permitted by law. Where we do allow otherwise permitted sharing, we provide you the opportunity to opt out, and such instructions are described in the section of this notice advising your choices.  

We may, however, disclose your personal information to nonaffiliates in the course of our everyday business, to provide services to investors and purchasers in connection with the sale, transfer, securitization, or other disposition of your loan. We may also disclose your information to service providers that assist us in carrying out our business, as necessary to complete the transactions you request, to comply with legal requirements, or to protect your rights. Such disclosures are made consistent with applicable law and the confidentiality safeguards described in this notice.  

3. JOINT MARKETING ARRANGEMENTS  
A joint marketing arrangement is a formal agreement between nonaffiliated financial companies that we work with to provide financial products or services to you. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. works with other financial companies under a joint marketing arrangement in order to offer you financial products and services, and we share information with these companies for the purposes of the joint marketing to protect the confidentiality and security of your information.

Federal law does not give you the right to limit sharing for joint marketing of financial products and services, although you may have the right under applicable law to opt out of receiving certain marketing communications. If you prefer not to receive marketing offers resulting from a joint marketing arrangement, you may contact us using the information provided at the end of this notice, and we will take reasonable steps to honor your preference within the time permitted by law. When you are no longer our customer, we continue to share your information as described in this notice, subject to applicable law. Limiting sharing for joint marketing will not apply to disclosures made in our everyday business, to service your account, or to comply with our legal and regulatory obligations. 9: HOW WE PROTECT YOUR INFORMATION To protect your personal information from unauthorized access and use, we maintain physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards that comply with applicable federal and state standards. These measures include computer safeguards, secured files and buildings, and access control measures to detect and prevent unauthorized access to your information. Our training programs are also designed to inform employees about the importance of privacy and security and to establish procedures to protect your information. We restrict access to your nonpublic personal information to employees, agents, and service providers who need to know that information about you in order to provide products or services to you or for whom you have authorized us to disclose your information. When we engage third parties to perform services on our behalf, we will require them to protect the confidentiality of your information and to use it only for the purpose for which we disclose it. We will also take steps to help ensure that our procedures are followed and that our employees are trained to comply with our security standards, and we encourage you to protect your account credentials and to notify us promptly of any suspected unauthorized activity.

10. INFORMATION FOR FORMER CUSTOMERS Even after you cease to be a customer of Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., we continue to maintain and protect your personal information in accordance with this notice and applicable law. We retain your information for as long as necessary to service the loan during its life, to comply with our legal, regulatory, and recordkeeping obligations, to resolve disputes, and to enforce our agreements. NOTICE & DISCLOSURE
This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to William Gray by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in connection with loan number 4222315360 and the credit
transaction concerning the property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040. The federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act and its
implementing regulation, Regulation B, prohibit creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on certain prohibited bases and confer upon you important
rights with respect to the evaluation of your application, the disposition of your request for credit, and your access to the reasons underlying any action taken.
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is committed to extending credit to all creditworthy applicants in a fair, consistent, and lawful manner, and this disclosure summarizes
the protections, obligations, and remedies that govern the credit transaction described above as of February 23, 2024.
§ 1. PROHIBITED BASES OF DISCRIMINATION
The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B make it unlawful for any creditor, including Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., to discriminate against an applicant with
respect to any aspect of a credit transaction on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, or age, provided the applicant has the capacity to enter
into a binding contract. These prohibited bases extend to every phase of the credit relationship, including the taking and evaluation of applications, the establishment of
terms and conditions, the administration of accounts, and the collection of amounts owed. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. does not consider any prohibited basis as a negative
factor in its assessment of the creditworthiness of William Gray.
It is further unlawful for a creditor to discriminate against an applicant because all or part of the applicant's income derives from any public assistance program, or because
the applicant has in good faith exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. The federal agencies that enforce compliance with this law may, in
appropriate circumstances, consider a creditor's conduct toward applicants who exercise such rights. Accordingly, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. evaluates income from
public assistance and other lawful sources on the same terms applied to comparable income, and it does not penalize William Gray for asserting any statutory right.
These protections apply uniformly regardless of the type, size, or purpose of the credit requested, whether the extension of credit is secured or unsecured, and whether the
transaction is consummated, withdrawn, or denied. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. maintains internal policies, training, and monitoring designed to ensure that personnel
involved in the origination, underwriting, and servicing of credit adhere to these requirements at every stage of the transaction associated with loan number 4222315360.
§ 2. TREATMENT OF MARITAL STATUS
Consistent with Regulation B, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will not refuse to extend credit, nor will it terminate or unfavorably alter the terms of an existing extension of
credit, solely on the basis of the marital status of William Gray, except where such consideration is expressly permitted or required by applicable law. You may apply for
credit in your own name, and a creditor may not require the signature of a spouse or other person, other than a joint applicant, on any credit instrument if you qualify under
the creditor's standards of creditworthiness for the amount and terms of the credit requested.
Where the value of jointly owned property, or property located in a community property jurisdiction, is relied upon to satisfy the creditor's standards, additional signatures
may be required to the extent necessary to make the collateral available to satisfy the debt in the event of default, consistent with the law of the applicable VT jurisdiction.
Any such requirement will be applied without regard to sex or marital status and only to the extent reasonably necessary to protect the creditor's legitimate interest in the
collateral securing loan number 4222315360.
If you are married, you have the right to have credit history that reflects accounts you and your spouse use or for which you are both liable reported in the name of each
spouse. Upon request, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will furnish information to consumer reporting agencies in a manner that enables William Gray to establish an
independent credit history, subject to the accuracy of the underlying account records and the requirements of applicable law.
§ 3. NOTIFICATION OF ACTION TAKEN
Regulation B requires Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to notify William Gray of the action taken on an application for credit within the time periods prescribed by law,
generally within thirty (30) days after receiving a completed application concerning the credit requested. The action taken may consist of an approval, a counteroffer, an
approval subject to conditions, an incomplete-application notice, or a denial. Where a counteroffer is extended and you do not expressly accept or use the credit offered,
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may treat the application in accordance with the timing and notice rules established under Regulation B.
If an application is incomplete regarding matters that William Gray can complete, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may, in lieu of providing a notice of action taken, send a
written notice specifying the information needed, designating a reasonable period of time within which to furnish it, and informing you that failure to provide the requested
information will result in no further consideration of the application. The period afforded for completion will be reasonable under the circumstances and will be
communicated in the notice issued in connection with loan number 4222315360.
A notice of action taken will be provided in a form that you may retain, and it will identify Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. and contain the disclosures required by law. Nothing
in this notice limits any additional notification rights you may hold under other federal or VT consumer-protection statutes applicable to the credit transaction described
above.
§ 4. RIGHT TO A STATEMENT OF REASONS
When Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. takes adverse action on an application for credit, or on an existing account, you have the right under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to
a statement of the specific reasons for that action. Adverse action includes a denial or revocation of credit, a change in the terms of an existing account that is not favorable
to William Gray and that is not applied to all or substantially all of a class of the creditor's accounts, and a refusal to grant credit in substantially the amount or on
substantially the terms requested in an application.
The statement of specific reasons will either be provided automatically with the notice of adverse action or be made available upon your timely request. If the reasons are
not disclosed in the initial notice, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will include a statement of your right to request the reasons within sixty (60) days of the notification, together
Retain this document with your loan file records.

with the name, address, and telephone number of the person or office from which the reasons may be obtained. Upon a timely oral or written request, Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc. will furnish the specific reasons in writing within a reasonable period of time, generally not exceeding thirty (30) days.
The reasons disclosed will be specific and will indicate the principal factors that influenced the action taken; statements that the action was based on internal standards or
policies, or that the applicant failed to achieve a qualifying score, are not by themselves sufficient. Any statement of reasons furnished in connection with loan number
4222315360 will accurately reflect the actual basis upon which the credit decision was made.
§ 5. ADVERSE ACTION AND CREDIT SCORING
If Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. bases its credit decision in whole or in part on information obtained from a consumer reporting agency, the notice of adverse action provided
to William Gray will identify the agency that furnished the report, including its name, address, and telephone number, and will state that the agency did not make the credit

report.
Where the credit decision is based in whole or in part on a credit score obtained from a consumer reporting agency, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will disclose the score used,
the range of possible scores, the key factors that adversely affected the score, the date the score was created, and the name of the entity that provided the score, to the extent
required by applicable law. These disclosures are intended to enable William Gray to understand the role that scored information played in the action taken on the
application bearing loan number 4222315360.
If Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. obtains information from an outside source other than a consumer reporting agency, you have the right to make a written request, within
sixty (60) days of receiving the adverse-action notice, for disclosure of the nature of that information. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will respond to any such request in
accordance with the procedures and timing established under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and Regulation B.
§ 6. GOVERNMENT MONITORING INFORMATION
For applications to purchase or refinance a dwelling occupied or to be occupied as a principal residence, federal law requires Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to request certain
information regarding the applicant's ethnicity, race, and sex for the purpose of monitoring compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, the Home Mortgage
Disclosure Act, and other fair-lending statutes. The furnishing of this information by William Gray is voluntary, and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may not discriminate on
the basis of this information, or on the basis of whether you choose to provide it, in any aspect of the credit transaction.
If you choose not to furnish the requested monitoring information, federal regulations may require Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to note the information on the basis of visual
observation, surname, or both, for an application taken in person. This collection is undertaken solely for governmental monitoring and statistical purposes and plays no
part in the evaluation of the creditworthiness of William Gray or in the decision to extend, deny, or condition the credit associated with loan number 4222315360.
Monitoring information collected by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is maintained, reported, and safeguarded in accordance with applicable federal and VT law and is
segregated from the factors used in underwriting to the extent required. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will not use this information for any purpose inconsistent with the
regulatory objectives for which it is gathered.
§ 7. APPRAISALS AND VALUATIONS
In connection with an application for credit to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will provide William Gray with copies of all appraisals
and other written valuations developed in connection with the application, whether or not the credit is ultimately extended. Such copies will be furnished promptly upon
completion, or within the period prescribed by Regulation B before consummation or account opening, whichever is earlier, regardless of whether the application results in
a closed transaction.
You may waive the timing requirement for receiving these copies in advance, provided that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. delivers the copies at or before consummation,
except where the extension of credit is denied or the application is withdrawn, in which event copies will be delivered in accordance with the applicable regulatory
timeframe. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may charge William Gray a reasonable fee for the reasonable cost of the appraisal or valuation, but it may not charge a fee solely
for furnishing the required copies.
These appraisal and valuation rights apply to the property identified as 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040 and are intended to ensure that
William Gray has timely access to the information upon which the secured credit decision is based. Any questions concerning the valuations obtained in connection with
loan number 4222315360 may be directed to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at the contact information set forth below.
§ 8. INFORMATION REQUESTS AND OPTIONAL DISCLOSURES
In evaluating your application, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may request only the information that is permissible under Regulation B and that is reasonably necessary to
assess the creditworthiness of William Gray and the sufficiency of any collateral. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will not inquire about your race, color, religion, national
origin, or sex except for the limited monitoring purposes described above or as otherwise permitted by law, and it will not inquire about your marital status except to the

You are not required to disclose income derived from alimony, child support, or separate maintenance payments unless you wish to have that income considered in support
