P LAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. — NOTICE OF SERVICING TRANSFER
Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing
Rights
LOAN 4222315360 / PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. / 02/23/2024
This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to William Gray in
connection with loan number 4222315360 secured by the property located at 98296 Douglas
Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement
Procedures Act (RESPA) and its implementing regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan,
meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is being or may
be transferred. This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not
change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late-fee protection period
operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it
carefully and retain it with your permanent loan records.
1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and
process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest, and any escrowed amounts, is being
assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential
mortgage industry and reflects an arrangement between the prior servicer and Plaza Home Mortgage,
Inc.. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable
federal regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-
day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for
any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing mortgage documents. The
information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to
specify the effective dates relevant to that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations
of William Gray during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the terms
of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly
modified, continue to govern your loan.
2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is
the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and interest payments, managing any escrow
account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying
payments to your account, issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your
inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The servicer may
Retain this document with your loan file records.

or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and
the right to service it are distinct interests that may be held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which William Gray directs payments and account questions
changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The interest rate, the unpaid principal balance,
the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory
note and security instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore
administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom you
address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed
your loan documents.
3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of
your mortgage loan will be handled by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.. The prior servicer will cease to accept
payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date,
and Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have
received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law
in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two
notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any payment obligation has lapsed. Continue
to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and
contact Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 296.739.3411x317 if you have questions regarding which entity
should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.
4. IDENTITY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER
Your new servicer is Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence
concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it on and after the transfer date. The business
address for Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793,
and its toll-free or designated telephone number for borrower assistance is 296.739.3411x317.
Representatives are generally available during normal business hours to answer questions about your
account, your payment options, your escrow account, and the transition of servicing. When you contact
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., please reference loan number 4222315360 so that your account can be
located promptly.
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is committed to a seamless transition and to honoring the terms of your
existing loan. The records, payment history, and escrow information associated with your loan are being
transferred to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. so that it may administer the loan without interruption. If you
have established automatic payment arrangements, recurring electronic drafts, or bill-payment
instructions with your prior servicer, you should confirm with Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. whether those
arrangements have carried over or whether you must re-establish them, as such arrangements do not
always transfer automatically between servicers.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.
rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the address designated by Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc. for the receipt of payments, which will be set forth in your welcome correspondence and
on your periodic billing statements; general correspondence and account questions may be directed to
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891 Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793. To ensure
that your payments are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number
4222315360 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and follow any specific remittance
instructions provided by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc..
Payments received by your prior servicer after the transfer date will ordinarily be forwarded to Plaza
Home Mortgage, Inc., but you should not rely on such forwarding as a routine method of payment. To
avoid delays in the crediting of your account, send all payments due on or after the transfer date directly
to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.. If you remit payment through a third-party bill-payment service or your
own financial institution, promptly update the payee name, address, and account number on file with that
service so that your payments are routed to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. for all installments coming due
on and after the effective date of the transfer.
6. SIXTY-DAY LATE FEE PROTECTION
Federal law provides important protection to borrowers during a transfer of servicing. During the sixty
(60) day period beginning on the effective date of the transfer of servicing, a payment received by your
prior servicer on or before its due date, or within any applicable grace period, may not be treated as late
by either servicer, and no late fee or other penalty may be imposed on you, and no delinquency may be
reported to any consumer reporting agency, solely because the payment was sent to the prior servicer
rather than to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.. This protection is intended to guard against penalties that
could otherwise arise from confusion about where to send payments during the transition.
To benefit from this protection, you must still make each payment in full and on time according to the
schedule in your loan documents; the sixty (60) day window protects you only against being penalized
for sending an otherwise timely payment to the wrong servicer, and it does not excuse a payment that is
missed, made late, or made for less than the amount due. After the sixty (60) day period has elapsed, all
payments must be made to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in accordance with your loan documents, and
standard late charges and credit-reporting practices will apply to any payment that is not timely received
by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc..
7. YOUR CONTINUING PAYMENT OBLIGATION
The transfer of servicing does not affect, reduce, suspend, or otherwise alter your obligation to make
timely payments under your promissory note and security instrument. William Gray remains fully
responsible for paying all amounts due, including principal, interest, and any required escrow deposits,
on or before each scheduled due date, regardless of which entity is servicing the loan at the time a
payment comes due. The amount of your monthly payment, the application of payments to your
account, and all other contractual terms continue to be governed by your existing loan documents
except to the extent they may be modified in a separate writing signed by the appropriate parties.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

If at any time you are unable to make a scheduled payment, you should contact Plaza Home Mortgage,
Inc. promptly at 296.739.3411x317 to discuss the options that may be available to you. Do not allow
uncertainty arising from the servicing transfer to cause you to withhold or delay a payment, as the
protections described in this Notice are limited and do not relieve you of your underlying obligation to
pay. Keeping your account current throughout and after the transition is the most reliable way to preserve
your standing under the loan and to avoid the accrual of late charges or other consequences of
delinquency.
8. ESCROW ACCOUNT AND INSURANCE
If your loan includes an escrow account for the payment of property taxes, hazard insurance premiums,
mortgage insurance, or other charges, the administration of that account will transfer to Plaza Home
Mortgage, Inc. along with the servicing of your loan. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will continue to collect
escrow deposits as part of your monthly payment and to disburse funds from the escrow account to pay
covered items as they come due. Your escrow account balance, payment history, and any cushion
permitted by applicable law will be transferred to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., and an annual escrow
account statement will be furnished to you in accordance with applicable requirements.
You should review your insurance policies and tax bills to confirm that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is
correctly identified, where applicable, as the party to receive notices, and you should forward to Plaza
Home Mortgage, Inc. any tax bills or insurance notices that you receive directly. Maintaining required
hazard and, if applicable, flood insurance on the property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334,
New Kaylachester, VT 08040 remains your obligation under the security instrument. If required coverage
lapses, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may obtain lender-placed insurance as permitted by your loan
documents and applicable law, the cost of which may be charged to you and may be more expensive
and provide less coverage than a policy you obtain on your own.
9. NOTICES OF ERROR AND INFORMATION REQUESTS
If you believe an error has occurred in the servicing of your loan, or if you need information about your
loan, you have the right to submit a written notice of error or a written request for information to Plaza
Home Mortgage, Inc.. To be effective, such a notice or request should be sent in writing to the address
that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. designates for the receipt of error notices and information requests,
which may differ from the address used for payments; that designated address will be identified in your
statements and welcome materials, with general correspondence directed to 7891 Perez Mission Apt.
096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793. Your written communication should include your name, loan number
4222315360, and sufficient detail to enable Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to identify the account and the
specific error or information at issue.
Upon receiving a qualifying written notice of error or information request, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will
provide a written acknowledgment within the time required by applicable law and will conduct a
reasonable investigation or provide the requested information, or an explanation of why the information is
unavailable, within the applicable response period. While a covered error notice is being investigated,
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may not furnish adverse information to a consumer reporting agency
regarding any payment that is the subject of the notice, to the extent required by law. Telephone calls to
Retain this document with your loan file records.

customer service may help resolve routine questions, but to invoke these specific statutory protections
you must submit your notice or request in writing.
10. COMMUNICATIONS AND ACCOUNT INFORMATION
On and after the transfer date, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will send your periodic billing statements,
escrow statements, year-end tax and interest summaries, and other required disclosures relating to the
servicing of your loan. You should expect to receive a welcome package or similar correspondence from
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. that confirms your account number, payment address, due date, and the
methods available for making payments and obtaining account information. Please review all such
communications carefully, retain them with your records, and notify Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. promptly
of any discrepancy between the information shown and your understanding of the terms of your loan.
To help ensure that you receive important notices without interruption, keep your mailing address,
telephone number, and any email address on file with Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. current at all times. If
you have authorized electronic delivery of statements or other documents, confirm that your electronic
delivery preferences have been carried over to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. or re-establish them as
necessary. Should you have any question about the authenticity of a communication that appears to
come from Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., you may verify it by contacting Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.
directly at 296.739.3411x317 before providing any personal or financial information in response.
11. EFFECT ON LOAN TERMS AND LEGAL RIGHTS
Except for the change in the identity of the servicer and the place to which payments and
correspondence are directed, the transfer of servicing has no effect on the terms or conditions of your
mortgage loan other than those directly related to the servicing of the loan. Your promissory note, your
security instrument, and any riders, addenda, or modifications thereto remain binding according to their
terms. The transfer does not constitute a refinancing, a novation, or a waiver of any right or remedy
available to the holder of the loan or to William Gray, and it does not enlarge or diminish any defense or
claim that either party may otherwise possess.
This Notice is provided for informational purposes and to satisfy applicable disclosure obligations; it is
not legal or financial advice. If you have questions about how the transfer of servicing affects your
particular circumstances, or about your rights and obligations under federal or VT law, you may wish to
consult an attorney or a qualified housing counselor. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will administer your loan
in accordance with the loan documents and applicable law, and it will make available to you, upon
reasonable request, information necessary to understand the status and handling of your account
following the transfer.

William Gray

                       RESIDENTIAL PURCHASE AND SALE AGREEMENT

THIS RESIDENTIAL PURCHASE AND SALE AGREEMENT is made and entered into as of November 16, 2023, by and between William Gray ("BUYER") and Courtney Henry ("SELLER"). BUYER agrees to buy and SELLER agrees to sell the real property described below upon the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.

1. Parties
   BUYER:            William Gray
   SELLER:          Courtney Henry
   Listing Agent:    Meghan Mendez – Sanders Group
   Listing Agent:    Debra White – Martinez, Conrad and Garcia

2. Property
   Address:        89209 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kayshaker, VT 09840
   County:         Bell County
   Parcel / APN:   27-740-835
   Community:      Valencia Trails
   Legal/Unit:     0181 – Flamingo #494

3. Purchase Price
   BASIC PRICE:                     $749,230.00      LATE PENALTY:        $35,000.00
   TOTAL PURCHASE PRICE:          $796,318.00

   1. The total purchase price for the Property shall be $796,318.00 payable by BUYER as follows: an earnest money deposit of $5,000.00 to be held by Nova Title Company and credited toward the purchase price at closing, plus additional cash at closing of $791,318.00.
   2. Buyer intends to finance the Property with a loan of $750,000.00.
   3. Buyer has deposited $5,000.00 with the Title Company on __________.
  
4. Closing 
   Buyer shall close on or before March 24, 2024 (or as otherwise agreed to by the parties of Nova Title Company), at such other time as mutually agreed. At closing, all taxes shall be prorated; Seller shall pay all applicable closing costs. 
    
5. Financing Contingency: This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER obtaining a firm written loan commitment in the amount of $750,000.00 through a lender. BUYER shall provide written notification of obtaining such loan approval or denial within 15 days following the execution of this contract; if unable to obtain such approval, this Agreement shall be deemed null and void and any earnest money will be refunded.

INSPECTION CONTINGENCY. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER's right, at BUYER's expense, to have the Property inspected by licensed inspectors of BUYER's choosing. Should inspection reveal material defects, BUYER may request repairs, renegotiate the price, or terminate this Agreement within the inspection period.

TITLE CONTINGENCY. SELLER shall convey good and marketable title by general warranty deed, free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except those of record accepted by BUYER. Title shall be insured through Nova Title Company. BUYER may terminate this Agreement if title defects are not timely cured.

DEFAULT. If BUYER defaults, SELLER may retain the earnest money as liquidated damages. If SELLER defaults, BUYER may pursue specific performance or recover the earnest money together with any actual damages permitted by law.

ACCEPTANCE AND SIGNATURES

The parties acknowledge that they have read and understood this Agreement and agree to be bound by its terms. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties.

William Gray

From: Albert Yates <campbellsharon@example.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 29, 2024 12:00 AM
To: William Gray <william.gray@example.com>
Subject: Conditional Approval - Loan #4222315360 - William Gray

Dear William Gray,
Good news - your mortgage application (loan #4222315360), submitted on 02/23/2024, has been conditionally approved. Before we can move forward with underwriting and clear-to-close, we need your employment and paystubs. 

Please provide the items listed below. The two priorities are an updated paystub and a completed written Verification of Employment (VOE) from Rivera PLC.

PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC. - GENERAL DISCLOSURES AND BORROWER
ACKNOWLEDGMENTS
General Disclosures and Acknowledgments
Issued to WIllIam Gray

LENDER Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.
LOAN NO. 4222315360
DATE 02/23/2024
BORROWER William Gray

This consolidated General Disclosures and Borrower Acknowledgments document is furnished by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to William Gray in connection with
the loan identified as 4222315360 and secured by the real property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040 in the State of VT.
The disclosures and certifications set forth herein are provided to satisfy applicable federal and state requirements and to memorialize the understandings of the
parties as of February 23, 2024. By reviewing and executing this document, William Gray acknowledges receipt of the notices described below and affirms the
accuracy of the representations made in the loan application and supporting materials. Capitalized placeholder terms are completed elsewhere within the loan
package and incorporated herein by reference for all purposes.
§ 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
This document consolidates several distinct borrower notices, certifications, and acknowledgments that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is required or elects to provide in
connection with the origination of the loan referenced above. It is intended to operate together with, and not in substitution for, the promissory note, security instrument,
and all other disclosures delivered to William Gray. Where any provision of this document conflicts with a more specific disclosure mandated by applicable law, the more
specific disclosure shall control to the extent of the conflict, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect without impairment.
Each section below addresses a separate subject matter and may be relied upon independently. The headings are provided solely for convenience and ease of reference and
do not limit, expand, or otherwise affect the substance of the acknowledgments that follow. William Gray understands that signing this document does not obligate Plaza
Home Mortgage, Inc. to close or fund the loan and does not waive any condition that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may impose prior to closing. Nothing herein creates any
commitment except as expressly stated in a separately executed written commitment from Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc..
William Gray acknowledges that this document has been made available in a manner permitting review prior to execution, and that William Gray has had a reasonable
opportunity to read it, to ask questions of Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., and to seek independent advice from counsel or other advisors of William Gray's choosing. The
execution of this document reflects William Gray's informed and voluntary agreement to the matters described herein as of the date set forth above.
§ 2. RIGHT TO RECEIVE APPRAISAL
In connection with an application for a loan to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is required to provide William Gray with a copy of each
written appraisal and valuation developed in connection with the application. William Gray will be provided a copy promptly upon completion, or no later than the period
required by applicable law before the loan closes, whichever is earlier, even if the transaction does not close or is not consummated. This right exists regardless of whether
William Gray requests a copy and is provided at no additional charge for the copy itself.
William Gray may be charged for the reasonable cost of any appraisal or valuation obtained, and such cost may be disclosed separately within the closing documents and
settlement statement applicable to the loan. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may require William Gray to pay for an appraisal as a condition of processing the application.
Receipt of a copy of any appraisal does not entitle William Gray to rely upon it for any purpose other than the credit transaction, and the appraisal remains the property of
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. unless otherwise required by law.
By executing this document, William Gray acknowledges that the right to receive a copy of any appraisal or valuation has been disclosed and understood. William Gray
may obtain additional information regarding this right by contacting Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 296.739.3411x317 or by writing to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. at 7891
Perez Mission Apt. 096, South Brendamouth, HI 02793. William Gray further understands that William Gray may waive the timing requirement for delivery in certain
circumstances permitted by law, provided any such waiver is made in a separate writing and does not affect William Gray's underlying right to receive the copy.
§ 3. ACCURACY OF INFORMATION
William Gray certifies that all statements, documents, and information provided to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in connection with the loan application, including but not
limited to information regarding income, employment, assets, liabilities, credit history, and the subject property, are true, complete, and accurate as of the date provided.
William Gray understands that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is relying upon the accuracy of this information in evaluating the application, in making its credit decision, and
in determining the terms upon which the loan may be offered. Any material misstatement or omission may constitute grounds for denial or for declaring the loan in default
after closing.
William Gray agrees to notify Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. promptly, in writing, of any material change in the information previously furnished that occurs at any time
before the loan is closed and funded. Such changes include, without limitation, a change in employment status, a material reduction in income, the incurrence of new debt,
or a change in the intended use or occupancy of the subject property. William Gray acknowledges that Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may verify any information provided
through third parties, consumer reporting agencies, employers, financial institutions, and other appropriate sources at any time.
William Gray acknowledges that intentionally or negligently making a false statement to Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. or to a federally related mortgage lender may
constitute a violation of applicable federal and state law and may subject William Gray to civil and criminal penalties, including fines and imprisonment. William Gray
affirms that William Gray has reviewed the information submitted and that it accurately reflects William Gray's financial condition and the facts surrounding the requested
transaction as of February 23, 2024.
§ 4. OCCUPANCY CERTIFICATION
William Gray certifies the intended occupancy status of the subject property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040 as represented in the
loan application. If the property has been represented as William Gray's principal residence, William Gray certifies an intention to occupy, establish, and use the property as
William Gray's principal residence within the period customarily required following closing, and to continue to occupy the property as a principal residence for the period
required by the loan documents, unless otherwise agreed by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in writing or required by extenuating circumstances beyond William Gray's
reasonable control.

EQUAL CREDIT OPPORTUNITY ACT NOTICE — PLAZA HOME MORTGAGE, INC.
Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice and Fair Lending
Disclosure
Issued to WIllIam Gray

LENDER Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc.
LOAN NO. 4222315360
DATE 02/23/2024
BORROWER William Gray

This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to William Gray by Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. in connection with loan number 4222315360 and the credit
transaction concerning the property located at 98296 Douglas Branch Apt. 334, New Kaylachester, VT 08040. The federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act and its
implementing regulation, Regulation B, prohibit creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on certain prohibited bases and confer upon you important
rights with respect to the evaluation of your application, the disposition of your request for credit, and your access to the reasons underlying any action taken.
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. is committed to extending credit to all creditworthy applicants in a fair, consistent, and lawful manner, and this disclosure summarizes
the protections, obligations, and remedies that govern the credit transaction described above as of February 23, 2024.
§ 1. PROHIBITED BASES OF DISCRIMINATION
The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B make it unlawful for any creditor, including Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc., to discriminate against an applicant with
respect to any aspect of a credit transaction on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, or age, provided the applicant has the capacity to enter
into a binding contract. These prohibited bases extend to every phase of the credit relationship, including the taking and evaluation of applications, the establishment of
terms and conditions, the administration of accounts, and the collection of amounts owed. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. does not consider any prohibited basis as a negative
factor in its assessment of the creditworthiness of William Gray.
It is further unlawful for a creditor to discriminate against an applicant because all or part of the applicant's income derives from any public assistance program, or because
the applicant has in good faith exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. The federal agencies that enforce compliance with this law may, in
appropriate circumstances, consider a creditor's conduct toward applicants who exercise such rights. Accordingly, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. evaluates income from
public assistance and other lawful sources on the same terms applied to comparable income, and it does not penalize William Gray for asserting any statutory right.
These protections apply uniformly regardless of the type, size, or purpose of the credit requested, whether the extension of credit is secured or unsecured, and whether the
transaction is consummated, withdrawn, or denied. Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. maintains internal policies, training, and monitoring designed to ensure that personnel
involved in the origination, underwriting, and servicing of credit adhere to these requirements at every stage of the transaction associated with loan number 4222315360.
§ 2. TREATMENT OF MARITAL STATUS
Consistent with Regulation B, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will not refuse to extend credit, nor will it terminate or unfavorably alter the terms of an existing extension of
credit, solely on the basis of the marital status of William Gray, except where such consideration is expressly permitted or required by applicable law. You may apply for
credit in your own name, and a creditor may not require the signature of a spouse or other person, other than a joint applicant, on any credit instrument if you qualify under
the creditor's standards of creditworthiness for the amount and terms of the credit requested.
Where the value of jointly owned property, or property located in a community property jurisdiction, is relied upon to satisfy the creditor's standards, additional signatures
may be required to the extent necessary to make the collateral available to satisfy the debt in the event of default, consistent with the law of the applicable VT jurisdiction.
Any such requirement will be applied without regard to sex or marital status and only to the extent reasonably necessary to protect the creditor's legitimate interest in the
collateral securing loan number 4222315360.
If you are married, you have the right to have credit history that reflects accounts you and your spouse use or for which you are both liable reported in the name of each
spouse. Upon request, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will furnish information to consumer reporting agencies in a manner that enables William Gray to establish an
independent credit history, subject to the accuracy of the underlying account records and the requirements of applicable law.
§ 3. NOTIFICATION OF ACTION TAKEN
Regulation B requires Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. to notify William Gray of the action taken on an application for credit within the time periods prescribed by law,
generally within thirty (30) days after receiving a completed application concerning the credit requested. The action taken may consist of an approval, a counteroffer, an
approval subject to conditions, an incomplete-application notice, or a denial. Where a counteroffer is extended and you do not expressly accept or use the credit offered,
Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may treat the application in accordance with the timing and notice rules established under Regulation B.
If an application is incomplete regarding matters that William Gray can complete, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. may, in lieu of providing a notice of action taken, send a
written notice specifying the information needed, designating a reasonable period of time within which to furnish it, and informing you that failure to provide the requested
information will result in no further consideration of the application. The period afforded for completion will be reasonable under the circumstances and will be
communicated in the notice issued in connection with loan number 4222315360.
A notice of action taken will be provided in a form that you may retain, and it will identify Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. and contain the disclosures required by law. Nothing
in this notice limits any additional notification rights you may hold under other federal or VT consumer-protection statutes applicable to the credit transaction described
above.
§ 4. RIGHT TO A STATEMENT OF REASONS
When Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. takes adverse action on an application for credit, or on an existing account, you have the right under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to
a statement of the specific reasons for that action. Adverse action includes a denial or revocation of credit, a change in the terms of an existing account that is not favorable
to William Gray and that is not applied to all or substantially all of a class of the creditor's accounts, and a refusal to grant credit in substantially the amount or on
substantially the terms requested in an application.
The statement of specific reasons will either be provided automatically with the notice of adverse action or be made available upon your timely request. If the reasons are
not disclosed in the initial notice, Plaza Home Mortgage, Inc. will include a statement of your right to request the reasons within sixty (60) days of the notification, together
